notes: SKILL.md fix (iteration-2): added 'Name tools and systems verbatim' instruction. org-chaos with_skill improved from 4/5 (0.80) to 5/5 (1.00) — CircleCI and GitHub Actions now named explicitly in the report.
with_skill mean pass_rate: 0.92 (down from 0.96 in iteration-1). Two new failures emerged.

startup-chaos with_skill: assertion 1 failed — 'must give at least one short-term action item related to testing'. The skill correctly prioritizes monitoring before testing for zero-test teams, but the assertion expects testing in the short-term horizon. This assertion may be too strict, or the skill needs a rule for zero-test baselines.

high-functioning with_skill: assertion 4 failed — 'must acknowledge the low incident rate of 0.1 P1/month'. The report says 'low incidents' but does not cite the specific figure. Same root cause as the tool-naming fix: specific user-provided metrics should be cited verbatim.

without_skill mean pass_rate: 0.76 (down from 1.00 in iteration-1). The without_skill agents produced shorter, less-structured reports this run. The high-functioning without_skill report was truncated and lacked a recommendations section entirely, causing 2 failures.

with_skill uses ~72% more tokens than without_skill (23346 vs 13585) due to loading 4 reference files (SKILL.md, checklist.md, red-flags.md, strategy.md). This is the expected cost of a structured skill.
with_skill is ~19.5s faster on average (89.82s vs 109.35s). Structured instructions produce more direct, focused output.

Highest-priority findings for iteration 3: (1) add instruction to cite user-provided metrics verbatim (not just tools), generalizing the tool-naming fix; (2) clarify that testing is always a short-term item when the team has zero or near-zero tests.
